name: homemade dukkah
notes:
STUB — to be expanded. Dukkah is a middle-eastern dry spice/seed/nut blend used as a finishing sprinkle. Canonical components are toasted nuts (hazelnuts, almonds, or pistachios), toasted seeds (cumin, coriander, sesame, sometimes fennel), and salt/pepper. The components are toasted separately, then coarse-ground together so the blend keeps texture rather than turning into a powder.

Used as a finishing sprinkle on bean salads, hummus, roasted vegetables, eggs, and yogurt dips. The white-bean-zaatar-salad recipe in this cookbook uses dukkah as a structural finish.

WANT TO TRY: a homemade version. Store-bought dukkah works but the homemade version is reportedly noticeably better.
